questions.question: During the simulation, Optics Focus is increased from 70% to 95% while all other parameters remain constant. Which outcome is most likely?

answers.c: Inspection Accuracy and AI Confidence Score increase

explanations.a: Incorrect. Conveyor speed is controlled independently and does not change with optics focus.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Product size is a user-defined parameter and is unrelated to optics focus.
explanations.c: Correct. Higher optics focus provides better image clarity and more reliable feature extraction, leading to improved inspection accuracy and higher AI confidence.
explanations.d: Incorrect. The defect threshold remains unchanged unless modified by the user.

questions.question: The simulator reports a high number of False Positives. What does this indicate?

answers.a: Many good products are incorrectly classified as defective
answers.c: Many defective products are correctly detected

explanations.a: Correct. False Positives occur when acceptable products are mistakenly rejected, increasing production cost and material waste.
explanations.b: Incorrect. False Positive values can only exist if products are being inspected.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Correctly identifying defective products represents True Positives.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Conveyor operation is unrelated to the definition of False Positives.

questions.question: Inspection Speed is increased significantly while the image processing capability remains unchanged. Which effect is most likely?

answers.c: Every product is rejected regardless of quality
answers.d: Inspection Throughput increases, but inspection accuracy may decrease

explanations.a: Incorrect. Camera resolution is determined by hardware specifications.
explanations.b: Incorrect. AI confidence depends on image quality and model performance, not inspection speed alone.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Product rejection depends on detected defects and decision thresholds.
explanations.d: Correct. Higher inspection speed improves throughput but may reduce the time available for image analysis, potentially lowering inspection accuracy.

questions.question: A product has a defect size below the selected Defect Threshold. How should the AI inspection system classify the product?

explanations.a: Incorrect. Defect threshold settings do not indicate system failure.
explanations.b: Correct. If the detected defect remains within the allowable threshold, the product satisfies the quality requirement and is accepted.
explanations.c: Incorrect. The AI system is designed to make a clear inspection decision.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Products are rejected only when the detected defect exceeds the permissible threshold.

questions.question: The AI inspection system reports a Precision of 98% and a Recall of 62%. What is the most appropriate interpretation?

answers.a: The conveyor speed is too low
answers.b: The inspection system is completely inaccurate
answers.d: Most rejected products are truly defective, but some defective products are still being missed

explanations.a: Incorrect. Precision and Recall evaluate AI detection performance rather than conveyor speed.
explanations.b: Incorrect. The system demonstrates high precision and is not completely inaccurate.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Precision and Recall do not indicate that every product is defective.
explanations.d: Correct. High Precision means very few false positives, while lower Recall indicates that some defective products are escaping detection as false negatives.

questions.question: The AI inspection system reports a Recall of 95%. What does this indicate?

answers.a: The system successfully detects most of the defective products
answers.c: Inspection speed is at its maximum

explanations.a: Correct. A high Recall indicates that the AI system identifies most defective products, minimizing false negatives and reducing the chance of defective products reaching customers.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Recall measures the detection of defective products, not the acceptance rate.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Recall is an AI performance metric and is independent of inspection speed.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Camera resolution affects image quality but is not represented by Recall.

questions.question: The simulator displays a low F1 Score even though Precision is high. What is the most likely reason?

answers.a: The industrial camera has been switched off
answers.c: Recall is low, causing an imbalance between Precision and Recall
answers.d: The conveyor belt is moving too slowly

explanations.a: Incorrect. If the camera were switched off, inspection would not proceed normally.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Product size does not mathematically affect the F1 Score.
explanations.c: Correct. The F1 Score combines Precision and Recall. A low Recall significantly reduces the F1 Score even when Precision is high.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Conveyor speed does not directly determine the F1 Score.

questions.question: The AI Confidence Score suddenly decreases after reducing the lighting quality around the inspection station. What is the most appropriate explanation?

answers.b: Lighting determines the number of products on the conveyor
answers.c: Lighting automatically changes the product dimensions
answers.d: Poor lighting reduces image quality, making defect detection less reliable

explanations.a: Incorrect. Conveyor speed is controlled independently of lighting conditions.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Lighting does not influence the number of products entering the inspection station.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Lighting does not alter the physical dimensions of the product.
explanations.d: Correct. Poor illumination decreases image quality, making feature extraction and defect classification more difficult, which reduces AI confidence.

questions.question: The Defect Percentage increases from 3% to 12% during production. What is the most appropriate engineering interpretation?

answers.a: The AI inspection system has automatically become inaccurate
answers.b: Manufacturing quality has deteriorated and the production process should be investigated
answers.d: Every product should automatically be accepted

explanations.a: Incorrect. An increase in detected defects does not necessarily indicate that the AI system is inaccurate.
explanations.b: Correct. A significant increase in defect percentage suggests deterioration in manufacturing quality, machine condition, material quality, or process stability and requires further investigation.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Conveyor operation is unrelated to the defect percentage itself.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Products should continue to be evaluated according to the inspection criteria.

questions.question: Based on the simulation, which combination represents the most effective AI-based quality inspection system?

answers.a: Low Optics Focus, High False Negatives, and Maximum Inspection Speed
answers.b: Low Inspection Accuracy and Frequent Incorrect Product Classification
answers.c: High Defect Percentage with Low AI Confidence
answers.d: High Optics Focus, High Inspection Accuracy, High AI Confidence, Low Defect Percentage, and Balanced Inspection Speed

explanations.a: Incorrect. Low optics focus and high false negatives reduce product quality and allow defective products to pass inspection.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Low inspection accuracy and frequent misclassification are undesirable characteristics of any AI quality inspection system.
explanations.c: Incorrect. High defect rates combined with low confidence indicate poor inspection performance or manufacturing issues.
explanations.d: Correct. High-quality imaging, reliable AI predictions, accurate inspection, and balanced production speed together produce an efficient and dependable industrial quality inspection system.
